!=.description: Returns true if a1 is not equal to a2, false  otherwise.

<.description:
Returns true if a1 is smaller than a2, false  otherwise. 
 
 Note
 
 Only comparisons among two numbers or two strings are supported.

<=.description:
Returns true if a1 is smaller than or equal to a2, false  otherwise.
 
 Note
 
 Only comparisons among two numbers or two strings are supported.

==.description: Returns true if a1 is equal to a2, false  otherwise.

==>.description: Symbol used to separate input and output values in operator signatures.

>.description:
Returns true if a1 is greater than a2, false  otherwise. 
 
 Note
 
 Only comparisons among two numbers or two strings are supported.

>=.description:
Returns true if a1 is greater than or equal to a2, false  otherwise.
 
 Note
 
 Only comparisons among two numbers or two strings are supported.

all?.description: Applies predicate quot2 to each element of quot1 and returns true if all elements of quot1 satisfy predicate quot2, false  otherwise.
all?.name: all?

and.description: Returns true if bool1 is equal to bool2, false  otherwise.
and.name: and

any?.description: Applies predicate quot2 to each element of quot1 and returns true if at least one element of quot1 satisfies predicate quot2, false  otherwise.
any?.name: any?

append.description: Returns a new quotation containing the contents of quot with a appended.
append.name: append

apply.description: Returns a new quotation obtained by evaluating each element of quot in a separate stack.
apply.name: apply

apply-interpolate.description: The same as pushing apply and then interpolate on the stack.
apply-interpolate.name: apply-interpolate

args.description: Returns a list of all arguments passed to the current program.
args.name: args

avg.description: Returns the average of the items of quot.
avg.name: avg

base.description: Sets the numeric base used to represent integers.
base.name: base

base?.description: Returns the numeric base currently used to represent integers (default: ["dec"](class:kwd)).
base?.name: base?

bind.description: Binds the specified value (auto-quoted) to an existing symbol 'sym.
bind.name: bind

bitand.description: Computes the bitwise *and* of integer int1 and int2.
bitand.name: bitand

bitclear.description: Sets the bytes specified via their position in int1 through quot to 0.
bitclear.name: bitclear

bitflip.description: Flips the bytes specified via their position in int1 through quot.
bitflip.name: bitflip

bitor.description: Computes the bitwise *or* of integers int1 and int2.
bitor.name: bitor

bitparity.description: Calculate the bit parity in int1. If the number of 1-bits is odd, the parity is 1, otherwise 0.
bitparity.name: bitparity

bitset.description: Sets the bytes specified via their position in int1 through quot to 0.
bitset.name: bitset

bitxor.description: Computes the bitwise *xor* of integers int1 and int2.
bitxor.name: bitxor

boolean.description:
Converts a to a boolean value based on the following rules:
 
  * If a is a boolean value, no conversion is performed.
  * If a is null, it is converted to false .
  * If a is a numeric value, zero is converted to false , otherwise it is converted to true.
  * If a is a quotation or a dictionary, the empty quotation or dictionary is converted to false , otherwise it is converted to true.
  * If a is a string, the empty string, and "false" are converted to false , otherwise it is converted to true.
boolean.name: boolean

boolean?.description: Returns true if a is a boolean, false  otherwise.
boolean?.name: boolean?

capitalize.description: Returns a copy of 'sym with the first character capitalized.
capitalize.name: capitalize

case.description:
This operator takes a quotation containing _n_ different conditional branches. 
 
 Each branch must be a quotation containing two quotations, and it is processed as follows:
 
   * if quot1 evaluates to true, then the quot2 is executed.
   * if quot1 evaluates to false , then the following branch is processed (if any).
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program prints "Smaller than 3":
 
     (
        ((2  3) ("Greater than 3" put!))
        ((2 < 3) ("Smaller than 3" put!))
        ((true) ("Exactly 3" put!))
     ) case
case.name: case

chr.description: Returns the single character str obtained by interpreting int as an ASCII code.
chr.name: chr

compiled?.description: Returns true if the current program has been compiled.
compiled?.name: compiled?

crypto.aes.description: Encrypts or decrypts 'sym1 using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) in CTR mode, using 'sym2 as password.
crypto.aes.name: aes

define.description: Defines a new symbol 'sym, containing the specified value.
define.name: define

define-sigil.description: Defines a new sigil 'sym, containing the specified value (auto-quoted if not already a quotation).
define-sigil.name: define-sigil

defined-sigil?.description: Returns true if the symbol 'sym is defined, false  otherwise.
defined-sigil?.name: defined-sigil?

defined-symbol?.description: Returns true if the symbol 'sym is defined, false  otherwise.
defined-symbol?.name: defined-symbol?

dequote.description:
Pushes the contents of quotation quot on the stack.

 Each element is pushed on the stack one by one. If any error occurs, quot is restored on the stack.
dequote.name: dequote

dev?.description: Returns true if the current program is being executed in development mode.
dev?.name: dev?

dict.del.description: Removes 'sym from dict1 and returns dict1.
dict.del.name: del

dict.get.description: Returns the value of key 'sym from dictionary dict.
dict.get.name: get

dict.get-raw.description: Returns the value of key 'sym from dictionary dict, wrapped in a dict:rawval.
dict.get-raw.name: get-raw

dict.has?.description:
Returns true if dictionary dict contains the key 'sym, false  otherwise.
 
 
 Example
  
 The following program returns true:
 
     {true :a1 "aaa" :a2 false :a3} 'a2 dict.has?
dict.has?.name: has?

dict.keys.description: Returns a quotation containing all the keys of dictionary dict.
dict.keys.name: keys

dict.pairs.description:
Returns a quotation containing a quotation for each value/key pair (value first, key second) of dictionary dict. 
 
 
 Example

 A The following program returns ((1 "a") (2 "b")):
     {1 :a 2 :b} dict.pairs
dict.pairs.name: pairs

dict.pick.description:
Returns a new dictionary dict2 containing the elements of dict1 whose keys are included in quot.
 
 
 Example
  
 The following program returns {4 :a 7 :d}:
 
     {5 :q 4 :a 6 :c 7 :d "d" :a} ("a" "d") dict.pick
dict.pick.name: pick

dict.set.description: Sets the value of the 'sym of dict1  to a, and returns the modified dictionary dict.
dict.set.name: set

dict.set-sym.description: Sets the value of the 'sym of dict1  to 'sym (treating it as a symbol), and returns the modified dictionary dict.
dict.set-sym.name: set-sym

dict.type.description: Returns a string set to the type of dict (empty if the dictionary has no type).
dict.type.name: type

dict.values.description: Returns a quotation containing all the values of dictionary dict.
dict.values.name: values

dictionary?.description: Returns true if a is a dictionary, false  otherwise.
dictionary?.name: dictionary?

difference.description:
Calculates the difference quot3 of quot1 and quot2.

 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (2) on the stack:
 
     (1 2 "test") ("test" "a" true 1) difference
difference.name: difference

drop.description: Returns a quotation quot2 containing the remaining elements after the first _n_ values of the input quotation quot1, or an empty quotation if int is greater than the length of quot1.
drop.name: drop

encode-url.description: URL-encodes 'sym, encoding all special characters into URL-encoded characters.
encode-url.name: encode-url

escape.description: Returns a copy of 'sym with quotes and backslashes escaped with a backslash.
escape.name: escape

even?.description: Returns true if int is even, false  otherwise.
even?.name: even?

exit.description: Exits the program or shell with int as return code.
exit.name: exit

expect.description:
If the -d (--dev) flag is specified when running the program, validates the first _n_ elements of the stack against the type descriptions specified in quot1 (_n_ is quot1's length) and if all the elements are valid returns them wrapped in quot2 (in reverse order). If the -d (--dev) flag is not specified when running the program, no validation is performed and all elements are just returned in a quotation in reverse order. 

 
 Tips
 
 * You can specify a typed dictionary by prepending the type name with dict:. Example: dict:socket
 * You can specify two or more matching types by separating combined together in a logical type expression, e.g.: string|quot

 
 Example
 
 Assuming that the following elements are on the stack (from top to bottom): 
 
 1 "test" 3.4
 
 the following program evaluates to true:
 
 (int string num) expect (3.4 "test" 1) ==
expect.name: expect

expect-all.description: Assuming that quot is a quotation of quotations each evaluating to a boolean value, it pushes true on the stack if they all evaluate to true, false  otherwise.
expect-all.name: expect-all

expect-any.description: Assuming that quot is a quotation of quotations each evaluating to a boolean value, it pushes true on the stack if any evaluates to true, false  otherwise.
expect-any.name: expect-any

expect-empty-stack.description: Raises an error if the stack is not empty.
expect-empty-stack.name: expect-empty-stack

filter.description:
Returns a new quotation quot3 containing all elements of quot1 that satisfy predicate quot2.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (2 6 8 12) on the stack:
 
     (1 37 34 2 6 8 12 21) 
     (stackdup 20 < stack.swap even? and) filter
filter.name: filter

find.description:
Returns the index of the first element within quot1 that satisfies predicate quot2, or -1 if no element satisfies it.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 3 on the stack:
 
     (1 2 4 8 16) 
     (5 ) find
find.name: find

first.description: Returns the first element of quot.
first.name: first

flatten.description:
Flattens all quotations within quot1 and returns the resulting sequence quot2.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8) on the stack:
 
     (1 (2 3 4) 5 (6 7) 8) 
     flatten
flatten.name: flatten

float.description:
Converts a to a float value based on the following rules:
 
   * If a is true, it is converted to 1.0.
   * If a is false , it is converted to 0.0.
   * If a is null, it is converted to 0.0.
   * If a is a integer, it is converted to float value.
   * If a is a float, no conversion is performed.
   * If a is a string, it is parsed as a float value.
float.name: float

float?.description: Returns true if a is a float, false  otherwise.
float?.name: float?

foreach.description: Applies the quotation quot2 to each element of quot1.
foreach.name: foreach

format-error.description:
Formats the error dict:error as a string. 
 
 Example
 
 The following code: 
 
      (
        (
           {"MyError" :error "This is a test error" :message} raise
        ) 
        (format-error)
      ) try
 
 produces: "This is a test error"
format-error.name: format-error

from-json.description: Converts a JSON string into min data.
from-json.name: from-json

from-semver.description:
Given a basic [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant string (with no additional labels) str, 
it pushes a dictionary dict on the stack containing a **major**, **minor**, and **patch** key/value pairs.
from-semver.name: from-semver

from-yaml.description:
Converts a YAML string into min data.
 
 Note
 
 At present, only YAML objects containing string values are supported.
from-yaml.name: from-yaml

fs.absolute-path?.description: Returns true if 'sym is an absolute path.
fs.absolute-path?.name: absolute-path?

fs.append.description: Appends str1 to the end of file str2.
fs.append.name: append

fs.atime.description: Returns a timestamp corresponding to the time that file/directory 'sym was last accessed.
fs.atime.name: atime

fs.ctime.description: Returns a timestamp corresponding to the time that file/directory 'sym was created.
fs.ctime.name: ctime

fs.dir?.description: Returns true if the specified path 'sym exists and is a directory.
fs.dir?.name: dir?

fs.dirname.description: Returns the path of the directory containing path 'sym.
fs.dirname.name: dirname

fs.exists?.description: Returns true if the specified file or directory 'sym exists.
fs.exists?.name: exists?

fs.expand-filename.description: Returns the absolute path to the file name 'sym.
fs.expand-filename.name: expand-filename

fs.expand-symlink.description: Returns the absolute path to the symlink 'sym (an error is raised if 'sym is not a symlink).
fs.expand-symlink.name: expand-symlink

fs.file?.description: Returns true if the specified path 'sym exists and is a file.
fs.file?.name: file?

fs.filename.description: Returns the file name of path 'sym.
fs.filename.name: filename

fs.hidden?.description: Returns true if file/directory 'sym is hidden, false  otherwise.
fs.hidden?.name: hidden?

fs.join-path.description: Joins the strings contained in quot with /.
fs.join-path.name: join-path

fs.mtime.description: Returns a timestamp corresponding to the time that file/directory 'sym was last modified.
fs.mtime.name: mtime

fs.permissions.description: Returns the Unix permissions (expressed as a three-digit number) of file/directory 'sym.
fs.permissions.name: permissions

fs.read.description: Reads the file str and puts its contents on the top of the stack as a string.
fs.read.name: read

fs.relative-path.description: Returns the path of 'sym1 relative to 'sym2.
fs.relative-path.name: relative-path

fs.size.description: Returns the size in bytes of file/directory 'sym.
fs.size.name: size

fs.stats.description:
Returns a dictionary dict containing information on file/directory 'sym.
 
 Example
 
 Assuming that min is a file, the following:
 
 "min" fs.stats
 
 produces:
 
      {
        "min" :name
        16777220 :device
        50112479 :file
        "file" :type
        617068 :size
        755 :permissions
        1 :nlinks
        1496583112.0 :ctime
        1496584370.0 :atime
        1496583112.0 :mtime
      }
fs.stats.name: stats

fs.symlink?.description: Returns true if the specified path 'sym exists and is a symbolic link.
fs.symlink?.name: symlink?

fs.type.description: Returns the type of file/directory 'sym ("file" or "dir").
fs.type.name: type

fs.unix-path.description: Converts all backslashes in 'sym to slashes.
fs.unix-path.name: unix-path

fs.windows-path.description: Converts all slashes in 'sym to backslashes.
fs.windows-path.name: windows-path

fs.write.description: Writes str1 to the file str2, erasing all its contents first.
fs.write.name: write

gets.description: Reads a line from STDIN and places it on top of the stack as a string.
gets.name: gets

harvest.description:
Creates a new quotation quot2 containing all elements of quot1 except for empty quotations.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 2 3) on the stack:
 
     (1 () () () 2 () 3) 
     harvest
harvest.name: harvest

help.description: Prints the help text for 'sym, if available.
help.name: help

http.download.description: Downloads the contents of URL str1 to the local file str2.
http.download.name: download

http.get-content.description: Retrieves the contents of URL str1 as str2.
http.get-content.name: get-content

http.request.description:
Performs an HTTP request. Note that dict is can be a standard (untyped) dictionary but its fields will be validated like if it was a dict:http-request.

 
 Example
 
 The following code constructs dict and passes it to the **request** operator to perform an HTTP GET request to <http://httpbin.org/ip:
 
     {}
       "GET" 'method dict.get
       "http://httpbin.org/ip" 'url dict.set
     request
http.request.name: request

http.start-server.description:
Starts an HTTP server based on the configuration provided in dict.
 
 dict is a dictionary containing the following keys:
 
 address
 : The address to bind the server to (default: **127.0.0.1**).
 port
 : The port to bind the server to.
 handler
 : A quotation with the following signature, used to handle all incoming requests: [dict:http-request ⇒ dict:http-response](class:kwd)
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program starts a very simple HTTP server that can display the current timestamp or date and time in ISO 8601 format:
 
     ; Define the request handler
     (
       ; Assume there is a request on the stack, take it off and give it the name req
       :req
       ; Let's see what we got (print req to standard out)
       "THE REQUEST:" puts! req puts!
       ; The request is a dictionary, we retrieve the value for the key url, and give it the name url
       req /url :url
       "THE URL is '$1'." url quote ))
       ) case
       :body
       ; Prepare the response
       {} body 'body dict.set
       stack.dup puts!
     )
     ; The request handler is ready, give it the name handler
     :handler
     
     ; Create the parameter dictionary for the server
     {}
     handler 'handler dict.set
     5555 'port dict.set
     
     ; Start server
     "Server started on port 5555." puts!
     "Press Ctrl+C to stop." puts!
     start-server
http.start-server.name: start-server

http.stop-server.description: Stops the currently-running HTTP server. This operator should be used within an HTTP server handler quotation.
http.stop-server.name: stop-server

if.description: If quot1 evaluates to true then evaluates quot2, otherwise evaluates quot3.
if.name: if

import.description: Imports the a previously-loaded module 'sym, defining all its symbols in the current scope.
import.name: import

in?.description: Returns true if a is contained in quot, false  otherwise.
in?.name: in?

indent.description: Returns str containing 'sym indented with int spaces.
indent.name: indent

indexof.description: If str2 is contained in str1, returns the index of the first match or -1 if no match is found.
indexof.name: indexof

infix-dequote.description:
Dequotes quot using infix notation. 
 
 Note that no special operator preference is defined, symbols precedence is always left-to-right. However, you can use parentheses (quotes) to evaluate expressions before others.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 17 on the stack:

      (2 + (3 * 5)) infix-dequote

 while this program leaves 25 on the stack:
 
      (2 + 3 * 5) infix-dequote
infix-dequote.name: infix-dequote

insert.description: Inserts a as the value of the _n^th_ element quot1 (zero-based), and returns the modified copy of the quotation quot2.
insert.name: insert

integer.description:
Converts a to an integer value based on the following rules:
 
   * If a is true, it is converted to 1.
   * If a is false , it is converted to 0.
   * If a is null, it is converted to 0.
   * If a is an integer, no conversion is performed.
   * If a is a float, it is converted to an integer value by truncating its decimal part.
   * If a is a string, it is parsed as an integer value.
integer.name: integer

integer?.description: Returns true if a is an integer, false  otherwise.
integer?.name: integer?

interpolate.description:
Substitutes the placeholders included in str with the values in quot.
 
 Notes
 
 * If quot contains symbols or quotations, they are not interpreted. To do so, call apply before interpolating or use apply-interpolate instead.
 * You can use the $# placeholder to indicate the next placeholder that has not been already referenced in the string.
 * You can use named placeholders like $pwd, but in this case quot must contain a quotation containing both the placeholder names (odd items) and the values (even items).
 
 
 Example
  
 The following code (executed in a directory called '/Users/h3rald/Development/min' containing 19 files):
 
 "Directory '$1' includes $2 files." (sys.pwd (sys.pwd sys.ls 'fs.file? filter size)) apply interpolate
 
 produces:
 
 "Directory '/Users/h3rald/Development/min' includes 19 files."
interpolate.name: interpolate

intersection.description:
Calculates the intersection quot3 of quot1 and quot2.

 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 "test") on the stack:
 
     (1 2 "test") ("test" "a" true 1) intersection
intersection.name: intersection

io.ask.description: Prints str1 (prompt), reads a line from STDIN and places it on top of the stack as a string.
io.ask.name: ask

io.choose.description:
Prints str2, then prints all str1 included in the quotation prepended with a number, and waits from valid input from the user.
 
 If the user enters a number that matches one of the choices, then the corresponding quotation quot1 is executed, otherwise the choice menu is displayed again until a valid choice is made.
io.choose.name: choose

io.color.description: Enables or disabled color output in terminal (enabled by default).
io.color.name: color

io.column-print.description: Prints all elements of quot to STDOUT, in int columns.
io.column-print.name: column-print

io.confirm.description:
Prints str (prompt) appending " [yes/no]: ", reads a line from STDIN and:
 
  * if it matches /^y(es)?$/i, puts true on the stack.
  * if it matches /^no?$/i, puts false  on the stack. 
  * Otherwise, it prints Invalid answer. Please enter 'yes' or 'no':  and waits for a new answer.
io.confirm.name: confirm

io.debug.description: Prints a and a new line to STDOUT, if logging level is set to [debug](class:kwd) or lower.
io.debug.name: debug

io.error.description: Prints a and a new line to STDERR, if logging level is set to [error](class:kwd) or lower.
io.error.name: error

io.fatal.description: Prints a and a new line to STDERR, and exists the program with error code 100.
io.fatal.name: fatal

io.getchr.description: Reads single character from STDIN without waiting for ENTER key and places its ASCII code on top of the stack.
io.getchr.name: getchr

io.info.description: Prints a and a new line to STDOUT, if logging level is set to [info](class:kwd) or lower.
io.info.name: info

io.mapkey.description:
Maps the named key/key combination 'sym to the quotation quot, so that quot is executed when key 'sym is pressed. 

 
 Notes

 * At present, only the key names and sequences defined in the [minline](https://h3rald.com/minline/minline.html) library are supported.
 * The quotation will be executed by a copy of the min interpreter created when the mapping was defined. In other words, quotations executed by key bindings will not affect the current stack.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program:
 
     (clear) 'ctrl+l io.mapkey 
 
 causes the CTRL+L key to clear the screen.
io.mapkey.name: mapkey

io.notice.description: Prints a and a new line to STDOUT, if logging level is set to [notice](class:kwd) (default) or lower.
io.notice.name: notice

io.password.description: Reads a line from STDIN displaying \* for each typed character, and places it on top of the stack as a string.
io.password.name: password

io.putchr.description: Prints str to STDOUT without printing a new line (str must contain only one character).
io.putchr.name: putchr

io.unmapkey.description:
Unmaps a previously-mapped key or key-combination 'sym, restoring the default mapping if available.

 
 Notes

 * At present, only the key names and sequences defined in the [minline](https://h3rald.com/minline/minline.html) library are supported.
 * At present, all the default mappings of min are those provided by the [minline](https://h3rald.com/minline/minline.html) library.
io.unmapkey.name: unmapkey

io.warn.description: Prints a and a new line to STDERR, if logging level is set to [warn](class:kwd) or lower.
io.warn.name: warn

join.description: Joins the elements of quot using separator 'sym, producing str.
join.name: join

lambda.description:
Defines a new symbol 'sym, containing the specified quotation quot. Unlike with define, in this case quot will not be quoted, so its values will be pushed on the stack when the symbol 'sym is pushed on the stack.
 
 Essentially, this symbol allows you to define an operator without any validation of constraints and bind it to a symbol.
lambda.name: lambda

lambda-bind.description: Binds the specified quotation to an existing symbol 'sym which was previously-set via lambda.
lambda-bind.name: lambda-bind

last.description: Returns the last element of quot.
last.name: last

line-info.description: Returns a dictionary dict containing a **filename**, **line**, and **column** properties identifying the filename, line and column of the current symbol.
line-info.name: line-info

linrec.description:
Implements linear recursions as follows:
 
   1. Evaluates quot1.
     * If quot1 evaluates to true, then it evaluates quot2.
     * Otherwises it executes quot3 and recurses using the same four quotations.
   2. Finally, it executes quot4.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 120 on the stack, the factorial of 5:
 
      5 (dup 0 ==) 'succ (dup pred) '* linrec
linrec.name: linrec

load.description: Parses and interprets the specified min file 'sym, adding [.min](class:ext) if not specified.
load.name: load

load-symbol.description: Loads the contents of symbol 'sym from the [.min\_symbols](class:file) file.
load-symbol.name: load-symbol

loglevel.description:
Sets the current logging level to 'sym. 'sym must be one of the following strings or quoted symbols:
 
   * debug
   * info
   * notice
   * warn
   * error
   * fatal
 
 
 Note
 
 The default logging level is _notice_.
loglevel.name: loglevel

loglevel?.description: Returns the current log level (debug, info, notice, warn, error or fatal).
loglevel?.name: loglevel?

lowercase.description: Returns a copy of 'sym converted to lowercase.
lowercase.name: lowercase

map.description: Returns a new quotation quot3 obtained by applying quot2 to each element of quot1.
map.name: map

map-reduce.description:
Applies quot2 (map) to each element of quot1 and then applies quot3 (reduce) to each successive element of quot1. quot1 must have at least one element.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 35 on the stack:
 
     (1 3 5) 
     (stack.dup *) (+) map-reduce
map-reduce.name: map-reduce

match?.description:
Returns true if str2 matches str1, false  otherwise.
 
 Tip
 
 str2 is a Perl-compatible regular expression
match?.name: match?

math.abs.description: Calculates tbe absolute value of num1.
math.abs.name: abs

math.acos.description: Calculates the arc cosine of num1 (in radians).
math.acos.name: acos

math.asin.description: Calculates the arc sine of num1 (in radians).
math.asin.name: asin

math.atan.description: Calculates the arc tangent of num1 (in radians).
math.atan.name: atan

math.ceil.description: Returns the smallest integer int that is not smaller than num.
math.ceil.name: ceil

math.cos.description: Calculates the cosine of num1 (in radians).
math.cos.name: cos

math.cosh.description: Calculates the hyperbolic cosine of num1 (in radians).
math.cosh.name: cosh

math.d2r.description: Converts num1 from degrees to radians.
math.d2r.name: d2r

math.e.description: Returns the value of the _e_ constant (Euler's number).
math.e.name: e

math.floor.description: Returns the largest integer int that is not greater than num.
math.floor.name: floor

math.ln.description: Calculates the natural logarithm of num1.
math.ln.name: ln

math.log10.description: Calculates the common logarithm of num1.
math.log10.name: log10

math.log2.description: Calculates the binary logarithm of num1.
math.log2.name: log2

math.pi.description: Returns the value of the π constant.
math.pi.name: pi

math.pow.description: Computes num1 to power raised of num2.
math.pow.name: pow

math.r2d.description: Converts num1 from radians to degrees.
math.r2d.name: r2d

math.round.description: Rounds num1 to the int^th decimal place.
math.round.name: round

math.sin.description: Calculates the sine of num1 (in radians).
math.sin.name: sin

math.sinh.description: Calculates the hyperbolic sine of num1 (in radians).
math.sinh.name: sinh

math.tan.description: Calculates the tangent of num1 (in radians).
math.tan.name: tan

math.tanh.description: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of num1 (in radians).
math.tanh.name: tanh

math.tau.description: Returns the value of the τ constant (2π).
math.tau.name: tau

math.trunc.description: Truncates num to the decimal point.
math.trunc.name: trunc

med.description: Returns the median of the items of quot.
med.name: med

mod.description: Returns the integer module of int1 divided by int2.
mod.name: mod

net.accept.description: Makes dict:socket2 (server) accept a connection from dict:socket1 (client). Returns the client socket dict:socket1 from which it will be possible to receive data from.
net.accept.name: accept

net.connect.description:
Connects socket dict:socket to address str and port int.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following code shows how to send a message to a server running on localhost:7777. The message is passed as the first argument to the program.
 
     {} net.socket "localhost" 7777 net.connect :cli
     
     args 1 get :msg
     
     "Sending message \"$1\" to localhost:7777..." (msg) =  net.send
     
     "Done." puts!
     
     cli net.close
net.connect.name: connect

net.listen.description:
Makes socket dict:socket1 listen to the specified address and port. dict can be empty or contain any of the following properties, used to specify the address and port to listen to respectively.
 
 address
 : The address to listen to (default: **0.0.0.0**).
 port
 : The port to listen to (default: **80**).
 
 
 Example
 
 The following code shows how to create a simple server that listens on port 7777, prints data received from clients, and exits when it receives the string exit:
 
     {} net.socket {"127.0.0.1" :address 7777 :port} net.listen :srv
     
     "Server listening on localhost:7777" puts!
     
     {} net.socket :cli
     "" :line
     (line "exit" !=)
     (
       srv cli net.accept #cli
       cli net.recv-line @line
       "Received: $1" (line) = % puts!
     ) while
     
     "Exiting..." puts!
     
     srv net.close
net.listen.name: listen

net.recv.description: Waits to receive int characters from dict:socket and returns the resulting data str.
net.recv.name: recv

net.recv-line.description:
Waits to receive a line of data from dict:socket and returns the resulting data str. "" is returned if dict:socket is disconnected.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following code shows how to make a simple GET request to <http://httpbin.org/uuid to receive a random UUID and display its response:
 
 
     {} net.socket "httpbin.org" 80 net.connect :cli
    
     cli "GET /uuid HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: httpbin.org\r\n\r\n" net.send
   
     cli net.recv-line puts :line
     (line "\}" match not) 
     (
       cli net.recv-line puts @line
     ) while
net.recv-line.name: recv-line

net.send.description: Sends str to the connected socket dict:socket.
net.send.name: send

net.socket.description:
Opens a new socket.
 
 dict can be empty or contain any of the following properties, used to specify the domain, type and protocol of the socket respectively.
 
 domain
 : The socket domain. It can be set to one of the following values:
   
   *  **ipv4** (default): Internet Protocol version 4.
   *  **ipv6**: Internet Protocol version 6.
   *  **unix**: local Unix file (not supported on Windows systems).
 type
 : The socket type. It can be set to one of the following values:
  
   * **stream** (default): Reliable stream-oriented service or Stream Socket.
   * **dgram**: Datagram service or Datagram Socket.
   * **raw**: Raw protocols atop the network layer.
   * **seqpacket**: Reliable sequenced packet service.
 protocol
 : The socket protocol. It can be set to one of the following values:
 
   * **tcp** (default): Transmission Control Protocol.
   * **udp**: User Datagram Protocol.
   * **ipv4**: Internet Protocol version 4 (not supported on Windows systems).
   * **ipv6**: Internet Protocol version 6 (not supported on Windows systems).
   * **raw**: Raw IP Packets protocol (not supported on Windows systems).
   * **icmp**: Internet Control Message Protocol (not supported on Windows systems).
net.socket.name: socket

null?.description: Returns true if a is null, false  otherwise.
null?.name: null?

number?.description: Returns true if a is a number, false  otherwise.
number?.name: number?

odd?.description: Returns true if int is odd, false  otherwise.
odd?.name: odd?

one?.description: Applies predicate quot2 to each element of quot1 and returns true if only one element of quot1 satisfies predicate quot2, false  otherwise.
one?.name: one?

operator.description:
Provides a way to define a new operator (symbol, sigil, or typeclass) on the current scope performing additional checks (compared to define and define-sigil), and automatically mapping inputs and outputs.
 
 quot is a quotation containing:
 
 * A symbol identifying the type of operator to define (symbol, sigil, or typeclass).
 * A symbol identifying the name of the operator.
 * A quotation defining the signature of the operator, containing input and output values identified by their type and a capturing symbol, separated by the == symbol.
 * A quotation identifying the body of the operator.

 The main additional features offered by this way of defining operators are the following:

 * If in development mode (-d or --dev flag specified at run time), both input and output values are checked against a type (like when using the expect operator *and* automatically captured in a symbol that can be referenced in the operator body quotation.
 * The full signature of the operator is declared, making the resulting code easier to understand at quick glance.
 * An exception is automatically raised if the operator body pollutes the stack by adding or removing elements from the stack (besides adding the declared output values).
 * It is possible to use the return symbol within the body quotation to immediately stop the evaluation of the body quotation and automatically push the output values on the stack.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program defines a pow operator that calculates the power of a number providing its base and exponent, and handling some NaN results using the return symbol:

      (
        symbol pow
        (num :base int :exp == num :result)
        ( 
          (base 0 == exp 0 == and)
            (nan @result return)
          when
          (base 1 == exp inf == and)
            (nan @result return)
          when
          (base inf == exp 0 == and)
            (nan @result return)
          when
          exp 1 - :n
          base  (dup) n times (*) n times @result
        )
      ) ::
operator.name: operator

opts.description: Returns a dictionary of all options passed to the current program, with their respective values.
opts.name: opts

or.description: Returns true if bool1 or bool2 is true, false  otherwise.
or.name: or

ord.description: Returns the ASCII code int corresponding to the single character str.
ord.name: ord

parent-scope.description: Returns a dictionary dict2 holding a reference to the parent scope of dict1 or null if dict1 is global.
parent-scope.name: parent-scope

parse.description: Parses str and returns a quoted program quot.
parse.name: parse

parse-url.description: Parses the url str into its components and stores them into dict:url.
parse-url.name: parse-url

partition.description:
Partitions quot1 into two quotations: quot3 contains all elements of quot1 that satisfy predicate quot2, quot4 all the others.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 3 5) (2 4 6) on the stack:
 
     (1 2 3 4 5 6) 
     (odd?) partition
partition.name: partition

prefix-dequote.description:
Dequotes quot using prefix notation (essentially it reverses quot and dequotes it).
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 4 on the stack:

     (* 8 4) prefix-dequote
prefix-dequote.name: prefix-dequote

prepend.description: Returns a new quotation containing the contents of quot with a prepended.
prepend.name: prepend

product.description: Returns the product of all items of quot. quot is a quotation of integers.
product.name: product

prompt.description:
This symbol is used to configure the prompt of the min shell. By default, it is set to the following quotation:
 
     ("[$1]$$ " (sys.pwd) = sidebar%
 Example
 
 Publish symbol [my-local-symbol](class:kwd) to [global](class:kwd) scope:
 'my-local-symbol global publish
prompt.name: prompt

puts.description: Prints a and a new line to STDOUT.
puts.name: puts

quit.description: Exits the program or shell with 0 as return code.
quit.name: quit

quotation?.description: Returns true if a is a quotation, false  otherwise.
quotation?.name: quotation?

quote-map.description: Returns a new quotation quot2 obtained by quoting each element of quot1.
quote-map.name: quote-map

quotecmd.description: Creates a command with the value of str and wraps it in a quotation.
quotecmd.name: quotecmd

quoted-symbol?.description: Returns true if a is a quoted symbol, false  otherwise.
quoted-symbol?.name: quoted-symbol?

quotesym.description: Creates a symbol with the value of str and wraps it in a quotation.
quotesym.name: quotesym

raise.description: Raises the error specified via the dictionary dict:error.
raise.name: raise

random.description:
Returns a random number int2 between 0 and int1-1. 
 
 
 Note
 
 You must call randomize to initialize the random number generator, otherwise the same sequence of numbers will be returned.
random.name: random

randomize.description: Initializes the random number generator using a seed based on the current timestamp.
randomize.name: randomize

range.description: Takes a quotation quot1 of two or three integers in the form of *start*, *end* and an optional *step* (1 if not specified) and generates the sequence and returns the resulting quotation of integers quot2.
range.name: range

raw-args.description: Returns a list of all arguments and (non-parsed) options passed to the current program.
raw-args.name: raw-args

raw-get.description: Returns the _n^th_ element of quot (zero-based) wrapped in a dict:rawval.
raw-get.name: raw-get

reduce.description:
Combines each successive element of quot1 using quot2. On the first iteration, the first two inputs processed by quot2 are a1 and the first element of quot1.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 120 on the stack:
 
     (1 2 3 4 5) 
     1 (*) reduce
reduce.name: reduce

reject.description: Returns a new quotatios quot3 including all elements of quot1 that do not satisfy predicate quot2 (i.e. the opposite of filter)
reject.name: reject

remove.description: Returns the _n^th_ element of quot1 (zero-based), and returns the modified copy of the quotation quot2.
remove.name: remove

remove-symbol.description: Removes the symbol 'sym from the [.min\_symbols](class:file) file.
remove-symbol.name: remove-symbol

repeat.description: Returns str containing 'sym repeated int times.
repeat.name: repeat

replace.description:
Returns a copy of str1 containing all occurrences of str2 replaced by str3
 
 Tip
 
 str2 is a Perl-compatible regular expression.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following:
 
 "This is a stupid test. Is it really a stupid test?" " s[a-z]+" " simple" replace
 
 produces:
 
 "This is a simple test. Is it really a simple test?"
replace.name: replace

replace-apply.description:
Returns a copy of str1 containing all occurrences of str2 replaced by applying quot to each quotation corresponding to each match.
 
 Tip
 
 str2 is a Perl-compatible regular expression.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following:
 
 ":1::2::3::4:" ":(\d):" (1 get :d "-$#-" (d) =%) replace-apply
 
 produces:
 
 "-1--2--3--4-"
 
 Note that for each match the following quotations (each containing the full match and the captured matches) are produced as input for the replace quotation: ("-1-" "1") ("-2-" "2") ("-3-" "3") ("-4-" "4")
replace-apply.name: replace-apply

require.description:
Parses and interprets (in a separated interpreter) the specified min module, and returns a module dictionary dict containing all the symbols defined in 'sym. 

This symbol will attempt to locate the specified module in this way. Given the following min program:

     'my-module require :my-module

1. Check for a file named my-module in the same folder as the current file (with our without a .min extension).
2. Check for a file named index.min in the mmm/my-module/*/index.min folder relative to the current file (locally-installed [managed-module](/learn-mmm)).
3. Check for a file named index.min in the $HOME/mmm/my-module/*/index.min folder (globally-installed [managed-module](/learn-mmm)). If multiple versions of the same module are present, the first one will be loaded.
require.name: require

rest.description: Returns a new quotation quot2 containing all elements of quot1 quotation except for the first.
rest.name: rest

return.description:
If used within the body quotation of an operator definition, causes the interpreter to stop pushing further body elements on the stack and start pushing tbe operator output values on the stack. 

If used outside of the body quotation of an operator definition, it raises an exception.
return.name: return

reverse.description: Returns a new quotation quot2 containing all elements of quot1 in reverse order.
reverse.name: reverse

save-symbol.description: Saves the contents of symbol 'sym to the [.min\_symbols](class:file) file.
save-symbol.name: save-symbol

saved-symbols.description: Returns a quotation containing all symbols saved in the [.min\_symbols](class:file) file.
saved-symbols.name: saved-symbols

scope.description:
Returns a dictionary dict holding a reference to the current scope.
  
 This can be useful to save a reference to a given execution scope to access later on.

 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves {(2) :two ;module} on the stack:
 
     {} :myscope (2 :two scope @myscope) -
scope.name: scope

scope-sigils.description: Returns a list of all sigils defined in dictionary dict.
scope-sigils.name: scope-sigils

scope-symbols.description: Returns a list of all symbols defined in dictionary dict.
scope-symbols.name: scope-symbols

seal-sigil.description: Seals the user-defined sigil 'sym, so that it cannot be re-defined.
seal-sigil.name: seal-sigil

seal-symbol.description: Seals symbol 'sym, so that it cannot be re-assigned.
seal-symbol.name: seal-symbol

sealed-sigil?.description: Returns true if the sigil 'sym is sealed, false  otherwise.
sealed-sigil?.name: sealed-sigil?

sealed-symbol?.description: Returns true if the symbol 'sym is sealed, false  otherwise.
sealed-symbol?.name: sealed-symbol?

search.description:
Returns a quotation containing the first occurrence of str2 within str1. Note that:
 
   * The first element of quot is the matching substring.
   * Other elements (if any) contain captured substrings.
   * If no matches are found, the quotation contains empty strings.
 
 
 Tip
 
 str2 is a Perl-compatible regular expression.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following:
 
 "192.168.1.1, 127.0.0.1" "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+)" search
 
 produces: ("192.168.1.1", "1", "1")
search.name: search

search-all.description: Returns a quotation of quotations (like the one returned by the search operator) containing all occurrences of str2 within str1.
search-all.name: search-all

semver-inc-major.description: Increments the major digit of the [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant string (with no additional labels) str1.
semver-inc-major.name: semver-inc-major

semver-inc-minor.description: Increments the minor digit of the [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant string (with no additional labels) str1.
semver-inc-minor.name: semver-inc-minor

semver-inc-patch.description: Increments the patch digit of the [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant string (with no additional labels) str1.
semver-inc-patch.name: semver-inc-patch

semver?.description: Checks whether str is a [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant version or not.
semver?.name: semver?

set.description: Sets the value of the _n^th_ element quot1 (zero-based) to a, and returns the modified copy of the quotation quot2.
set.name: set

set-sym.description: Sets the value of the _n^th_ element quot1 (zero-based) to 'sym (treating it as a symbol), and returns the modified copy of the quotation quot2.
set-sym.name: set-sym

shl.description: Computes the *shift left* operation of int1 and int2.
shl.name: shl

shorten.description: Returns a quotation quot2 containing the first _n_ values of the input quotation quot1.
shorten.name: shorten

shr.description: Computes the *shift right* operation of int1 and int2.
shr.name: shr

sigils.description: Returns a list of all sigils defined in the [global](class:kwd) scope.
sigils.name: sigils

slice.description:
Creates a new quotation quot2 obtaining by selecting all elements of quot1 between indexes int1 and int2.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (3 4 5) on the stack:
 
     (1 2 3 4 5 6) 
     2 4 slice
slice.name: slice

sort.description:
Sorts all elements of quot1 according to predicate quot2. 
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 3 5 7 9 13 16) on the stack:
 
     (1 9 5 13 16 3 7) ' sort
sort.name: sort

source.description: Display the source code of symbol 'sym (if it has been implemented a min quotation).
source.name: source

split.description: Splits 'sym1 using separator 'sym2 (a Perl-compatible regular expression) and returns the resulting strings within the quotation quot.
split.name: split

stack.cleave.description:
Applies each quotation contained in the first element to the second element a1.
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 2 on the stack:
 
 (1 2 3) ((sum) (size)) stack.cleave /
stack.cleave.name: cleave

stack.cons.description: Prepends a1 to the quotation on top of the stack.
stack.cons.name: cons

stack.dip.description: Removes the first and second element from the stack, dequotes the first element, and restores the second element.
stack.dip.name: dip

stack.dup.description: Duplicates the first element on the stack.
stack.dup.name: dup

stack.get.description: Puts a quotation containing the contents of the stack on the stack.
stack.get.name: get

stack.keep.description:
Removes the first element from the stack, dequotes it, and restores the second element.
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves 5 3 on the stack:
 
 2 3 '+ stack.keep
stack.keep.name: keep

stack.nip.description: Removes the second element from the stack.
stack.nip.name: nip

stack.over.description: Pushes a copy of the second element on top of the stack.
stack.over.name: over

stack.pick.description: Pushes a copy of the third element on top of the stack.
stack.pick.name: pick
stack.pick.signature: a1 a2 a3 ==> a1 a2 a3 a1

stack.pop.description: Removes the first element from the stack.
stack.pop.name: pop

stack.rolldown.description: Moves the third element in first position, the second in third position and the the first in second position.
stack.rolldown.name: rolldown
stack.rolldown.signature: a1 a2 a3 ==> a2 a3 a1

stack.rollup.description: Moves the third and second element into second and third position and moves the first element into third position.
stack.rollup.name: rollup
stack.rollup.signature: a1 a2 a3 ==> a3 a2 a1

stack.set.description: Substitute the existing stack with the contents of quot.
stack.set.name: set

stack.sip.description: Saves the quot1, dequotes quot2, and restores quot1.
stack.sip.name: sip

stack.spread.description:
Applies each quotation contained in the first element to each subsequent corresponding element.
 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (1 4) on the stack:
 
 (1 2) (3 4) ((0 get) (1 get)) stack.spread
stack.spread.name: spread

stack.swap.description: Swaps the first two elements on the stack.
stack.swap.name: swap

stack.swons.description: Prepends a1 to the quotation that follows it.
stack.swons.name: swons

store.delete.description: Removes an item from the datastore dict:datastore. The item is uniquely identified by 'sym, which contains the collection containing the item and the item id, separated by a forward slash (/). Puts the reference to the modified datastore back on tbe stack.
store.delete.name: delete

store.get.description: Retrieves item dict from datastore dict:datastore. dict is retrieved by specifying 'sym, which contains the collection containing the item and the item id, separated by a forward slash (/).
store.get.name: get

store.has?.description: Returns true if dict:datastore has a key called 'sym (which contains the collection containing the item and the item id), false  otherwise.
store.has?.name: has?

store.init.description: Initializes a bew datastore by creating the 'sym JSON file. Puts the datastore instance on the stack.
store.init.name: init

store.post.description: Adds the dictionary dict to the datastore dict:datastore inside collection 'sym, generating and adding a unique **id** field to dict. If the collection 'sym does not exist it is created. Puts the reference to the modified datastore back on tbe stack.
store.post.name: post

store.put.description: Adds the dictionary dict to the datastore dict:datastore. 'sym contains the collection where dict will be placed and the id of dict, separated by a forward slash (/). If the collection 'sym does not exist it is created. Puts the reference to the modified datastore back on tbe stack.
store.put.name: put

store.query.description:
Retrieves a quotation of dictionaries from the collection 'sym of datastore dict:datastore obtained by applying quot as a filter to each item of the collection, picking only the elements that match the filter.

 
 Example

 Assuming that **ds** is a datastore, the following program retrieves all elements of teh collection **posts** whose author field is set to "h3rald":

      ds "posts" ('author dget "h3rald" ==) dquery
store.query.name: query

store.read.description: Reads the previously-created datastore from the file 'sym and puts the resulting datastore instance on the stack.
store.read.name: read

store.write.description: Writes the contents of the datastore dict:datastore to the filesystem.
store.write.name: write

string.description: Converts a to its string representation.
string.name: string

string?.description: Returns true if a is a string, false  otherwise.
string?.name: string?

stringlike?.description: Returns true if a is a string or a quoted symbol, false  otherwise.
stringlike?.name: stringlike?

strip.description: Returns str, which is set to 'sym with leading and trailing spaces removed.
strip.name: strip

substr.description: Returns a substring str2 obtained by retrieving int2 characters starting from index int1 within str1.
substr.name: substr

sum.description: Returns the sum of all items of quot. quot is a quotation of integers.
sum.name: sum

symbols.description: Returns a list of all symbols defined in the [global](class:kwd) scope.
symbols.name: symbols

symmetric-difference.description:
Calculates the symmetric difference quot3 of quot1 and quot2.

 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (true "a" 2) on the stack:
 
     (1 2 "test") ("test" "a" true 1) symmetric-difference
symmetric-difference.name: symmetric-difference

sys.admin?.description: Returns true if the program is being run with administrative privileges.
sys.admin?.name: admin?

sys.chmod.description:
Sets the permissions of file or directory 'sym to int. int is a three-digit representation of user, group and other permissions. See the [Unix Permissions Calculator](http://permissions-calculator.org/) for examples and conversions.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program makes the file **/tmp/test.txt** readable, writable and executable by its owner, and readable and executable by users of the same group and all other users:
 
 "/tmp/test.txt 755 sys.chmod"
sys.chmod.name: chmod

sys.cp.description: Copies the file or directory 'sym1 to 'sym2.
sys.cp.name: cp

sys.cpu.description: Returns the host CPU. It can be one of the following strings i386, alpha, powerpc, powerpc64, powerpc64el, sparc, amd64, mips, mipsel, arm, arm64.
sys.cpu.name: cpu

sys.env?.description: Returns true if environment variable 'sym exists, false  otherwise.
sys.env?.name: env?

sys.hardlink.description: Creates hardlink 'sym2 for file or directory 'sym1.
sys.hardlink.name: hardlink

sys.ls.description: Returns a quotation quot containing all children (files and directories) of the directory 'sym.
sys.ls.name: ls

sys.ls-r.description: Returns a quotation quot containing all children (files and directories) of the directory 'sym, recursively.
sys.ls-r.name: ls-r

sys.mv.description: Moves the file or directory 'sym1 to 'sym2.
sys.mv.name: mv

sys.os.description: Returns the host operating system. It can be one of the following strings: windows, macosx, linux, netbsd, freebsd, openbsd, solaris, aix, standalone.
sys.os.name: os

sys.parent-dir.description: Returns the full path to the parent directory.
sys.parent-dir.name: parent-dir

sys.pwd.description: Returns the full path to the current directory.
sys.pwd.name: pwd

sys.rmdir.description: Deletes the specified directory 'sym and all its subdirectories recursively.
sys.rmdir.name: rmdir

sys.run.description: Executes the external command 'sym in the current directory without displaying its output. Returns a dictionary containing the command output and return code (in keys **output** and **code** respectively).
sys.run.name: run

sys.sleep.description: Halts program execution for int milliseconds.
sys.sleep.name: sleep

sys.symlink.description: Creates symlink 'sym2 for file or directory 'sym1.
sys.symlink.name: symlink

sys.system.description: Executes the external command 'sym in the current directory and pushes its return code on the stack.
sys.system.name: system

sys.unzip.description: Decompresses zip file 'sym1 to directory 'sym2 (created if not present).
sys.unzip.name: unzip

sys.which.description: Returns the full path to the directory containing executable 'sym, or an empty string if the executable is not found in **$PATH**.
sys.which.name: which

sys.zip.description: Compresses files included in quotation quot into zip file 'sym.
sys.zip.name: zip

take.description: Returns a quotation quot2 containing the first _n_ values of the input quotation quot1, or quot1 itself if int is greater than the length of quot1.
take.name: take

tap.description:
Performs the following operations:
 
 1. Removes a from the stack.
 2. For each quotation defined in quot (which is a quotation of quotations each requiring one argument and returning one argument):
    1. Pushes a back to the stack.
    2. Dequotes the quotation and saves the result as a.
 3. Push the resulting a back on the stack.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program:
 
     {1 :a 2 :b 3 :c} (
       (stack.dup 'a dict.get succ succ 'a dict.set)
       (stack.dup 'b dict.get succ 'b dict.set)
     ) tap
 
 Returns {3 :a 3 :b 3 :c}.
tap.name: tap

time.datetime.description: Returns an ISO 8601 string representing the combined date and time in UTC of timestamp int.
time.datetime.name: datetime

time.format.description:
Formats timestamp int using string str.
 
 
 Tip
 
 For information on special characters in the format string, see the [format](https://nim-lang.org/docs/times.html#format,TimeInfo,string) nim method.
time.format.name: format

time.info.description: Returns a timeinfo dictionary from timestamp int.
time.info.name: info

time.now.description: Returns the current time as Unix timestamp with microseconds.
time.now.name: now

time.stamp.description: Returns the current time as Unix timestamp.
time.stamp.name: stamp

time.to-timestamp.description: Converts the timeinfo dictionary dict:timeinfo to the corresponding Unix timestamp.
time.to-timestamp.name: to-timestamp

times.description: Applies the quotation quot int times.
times.name: times

titleize.description: Returns a copy of 'sym in which the first character of each word is capitalized.
titleize.name: titleize

to-bin.description: Converts int to its binary representation.
to-bin.name: to-bin

to-dec.description: Converts int to its decimal representation.
to-dec.name: to-dec

to-hex.description: Converts int to its hexadecimal representation.
to-hex.name: to-hex

to-json.description: Converts a into a JSON string.
to-json.name: to-json

to-oct.description: Converts int to its octal representation.
to-oct.name: to-oct

to-semver.description: Given a a dictionary dict containing a **major**, **minor**, and **patch** key/value pairs , it pushes a basic [SemVer](https://semver.org)-compliant string (with no additional labels) str on the stack.
to-semver.name: to-semver

to-yaml.description:
Converts a into a YAML string.

 
 Note
 
 At present, only min dictionaries containing string values are supported.
to-yaml.name: to-yaml

tokenize.description: Parses the min program str and returns a quotation quot containing dictionaries with a type symbol and a value symbol for each token.
tokenize.name: tokenize

try.description:
Evaluates a quotation as a try/catch/finally block. 
 
 The must contain the following elements:
 
 1. A quotation quot1 containing the code to be evaluated (_try_ block).
 1. _(optional)_ A quotation quot2 containing the code to execute in case of error (_catch_ block).
 1. _(optional)_ A quotation quot3 containing the code to execute after the code has been evaluated, whether an error occurred or not (_finally_ block).
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program executed on an empty stack prints the message "Insufficient items on the stack" and pushes 0 on the stack:
 
       (
         (stack.pop)
         (format-error puts)
         (0)
       ) try
try.name: try

type.description: Returns the data type of a. In cased of typed dictionaries, the type name is prefixed by dict:, e.g. dict:module, dict:socket, etc.
type.name: type

type?.description: Returns true if the data type of a satisfies the specified type expression 'sym, false  otherwise.
type?.name: type?

typealias.description: Creates a type alias 'sym1 for type expression 'sym2.
typealias.name: typealias

union.description:
Calculates the union quot3 of quot1 and quot2.

 
 Example
 
 The following program leaves (true 1 "test" "a" 2) on the stack:
 
     (1 2 "test") ("test" "a" true 1) union
union.name: union

unless.description: If 1 evaluates to false  then evaluates 2.
unless.name: unless

unseal-sigil.description: Unseals sigil 'sym, so that it can be re-defined (system sigils cannot be unsealed).
unseal-sigil.name: unseal-sigil

unseal-symbol.description: Unseals the user-defined symbol 'sym, so that it can be re-assigned.
unseal-symbol.name: unseal-symbol

uppercase.description: Returns a copy of 'sym converted to uppercase.
uppercase.name: uppercase

version.description: Returns the current min version number.
version.name: version

when.description: If quot1 evaluates to true then evaluates quot2.
when.name: when

while.description:
Executes quot2 while quot1 evaluates to true.
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program prints all natural numbers from 0 to 10:
 
     0 :count 
     (count 10 <=) 
     (count puts succ @count) while
while.name: while

with.description: Pushes each item of quot1 on the stack using the scope of quot2 as scope.
with.name: with

xml.cdata.description: Returns a dict:xml-cdata representing an XML CDATA section.
xml.cdata.name: cdata

xml.comment.description: Returns a dict:xml-comment representing an XML comment.
xml.comment.name: comment

xml.element.description: Returns a dict:xml-element representing an XML element (it will be an empty element with no attributes or children).
xml.element.name: element

xml.entity.description: Returns a dict:xml-entity representing an XML entity.
xml.entity.name: entity

xml.entity2utf8.description:
Returns the UTF-8 symbol corresponding to the specified XML entity. 
 
 
 Example
 
 The following program prints p to the screen:
 
      ">" xml.entity xml.entity2utf8 puts!
xml.entity2utf8.name: entity2utf8

xml.escape.description: Converts any <, `, &, ', and "` present in 'sym into the corresponding XML entities.
xml.escape.name: escape

xml.from-html.description: Returns an xml-node representing an HTML string (wrapped in a <document tag unless a valid HTML document is provided as input).
xml.from-html.name: from-html

xml.from-xml.description:
Returns an xml-node representing an XML string (element or fragment).
 
 
 Example
  
 The following program:

     "<a href='https://min-lang.org'min web site</a" from-xml  
 returns the following:
 
     {
       {"https://min-lang.org" :href} :attributes
      ({"min web site" :text}) :children
      "a" :tag
      ;xml-element
     }
xml.from-xml.name: from-xml

xml.query.description:
Returns an dict:xml-element representing the first element matching CSS the selector 'sym.
 
 
 Example
  
 The following program:

     "<ul
        <li class='test'first</li
        <li class='other'second</li
        <li class='test'third</li
     </ul" 
     xml.from-xml ".test" xml.query
 Returns the following:

     {
       {"test" :class} :attributes 
       ({"first" :text}) :children 
       "li" :tag 
       ;xml-element
     }
xml.query.name: query

xml.query-all.description:
Returns a list of dict:xml-element dictionaries representing all the elements matching CSS the selector 'sym.
 
 
 Example
  
 The following program:

     "<ul
        <li class='test'first</li
        <li class='other'second</li
        <li class='test'third</li
     </ul" 
     xml.from-xml ".test" xml.queryall
 Returns the following:

     ({
       {"test" :class} :attributes 
       ({"first" :text}) :children 
       "li" :tag 
       ;xml-element
     }
     {
       {"test" :class} :attributes 
       ({"third" :text}) :children 
       "li" :tag 
       ;xml-element
     })
xml.query-all.name: query-all

xml.text.description: Returns a dict:xml-text representing an XML text node.
xml.text.name: text

xml.to-xml.description: Returns a str representing an XML node.
xml.to-xml.name: to-xml

xor.description: Returns true if bool1 and bool2 are different, false  otherwise.
xor.name: xor
